name: TanStack Virtual (Headless UI Virtualization)

performance.notes: Only renders visible rows; constant DOM nodes regardless of list size

agent_readiness.notes: Headless virtualization for lists, grids, and tables. useVirtualizer({ count, getScrollElement, estimateSize }). Returns virtualItems array with index/start/size for each visible item. Works with fixed and dynamic/measured sizes. Horizontal, vertical, grid virtualization. Framework-agnostic core. No pre-built UI — full control over rendering. Pairs with TanStack Table for virtualized data grids. React, Vue, Solid, Svelte adapters. Alternative to react-window and react-virtualized (simpler API).

gotchas: Headless — must implement scroll container and item rendering yourself
Dynamic size measurement requires measurement ref pattern
No built-in infinite scroll (implement with intersection observer)
best_when: Lists or tables with thousands of rows needing smooth 60fps scroll
Custom data grids paired with TanStack Table
Any framework (React, Vue, Solid, Svelte) needing virtualization
Teams wanting simpler API than react-window
avoid_when: Need pre-built virtualized components (react-window has FixedSizeList)
Simple use cases where react-window's opinionated API is faster to implement
summary: TanStack Virtual is a headless virtualization library for rendering huge lists/tables at 60fps. useVirtualizer() returns virtualItems with positions for only visible rows. Framework-agnostic (React, Vue, Solid, Svelte). Fixed and dynamic sizes. Pairs perfectly with TanStack Table for virtualized data grids. More flexible but more work than react-window. Essential for any list/table with thousands of items.
